description: Build the hackathon-scoped Bidded swarm core: a Python + LangGraph worker backed by hosted Supabase that ingests a user-provided English text-PDF for a Swedish public procurement, seeds a larger IT consultancy profile, creates a validated shared evidence board from tender_document and company_profile sources, runs independent evidence-locked specialist motions before rebuttals, and persists an auditable bid/no-bid/conditional decision for a later Lovable UI.

userStories.title: Scaffold Python agent core
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Python agent-core scaffold so that Bidded has a testable home for Supabase access, LangGraph orchestration, PDF processing, and structured agent schemas.
acceptanceCriteria: Python package source lives under src/bidded with clear subpackages for config, db, documents, evidence, agents, orchestration, and cli.
Project dependencies include pytest, ruff, pydantic, python-dotenv or equivalent settings support, and placeholders for Supabase, Claude, LangGraph, PDF parsing, and optional embeddings.
.env.example documents ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, SUPABASE_URL, SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY, SUPABASE_STORAGE_BUCKET, and optional EMBEDDING_PROVIDER settings without committing secrets.
A minimal CLI entrypoint exists and can print help without connecting to Supabase or Claude.
pytest and ruff check pass for the scaffold.
userStories.notes: Added pyproject, src/bidded subpackages, Pydantic settings, CLI help, env/dependency docs, and deterministic pytest/Ruff coverage. Complete.

userStories.title: Add core Supabase schema
userStories.description: As a developer, I need core Supabase tables so that demo companies, procurement tenders, and uploaded documents have a stable storage contract before agent work begins.
acceptanceCriteria: SQL migrations create companies, tenders, and documents with UUID primary keys, created_at timestamps, and demo-tenant assumptions without auth/RLS.
documents store storage_path, checksum_sha256, content_type, document_role, parse_status, original_filename, and tender/company linkage where appropriate.
tenders store title, issuing authority, procurement context, language policy, and metadata JSONB for Swedish public procurement demo use.
companies can store the seeded larger IT consultancy profile with structured fields plus JSONB for fast-moving demo details.
Migration SQL is deterministic and can be applied to a hosted Supabase demo project.
userStories.notes: Added Supabase core domain migration for companies, tenders, and documents with demo tenant checks and deterministic contract tests. Complete.

userStories.title: Add agent audit schema
userStories.description: As a developer, I need agent run and audit tables so that every swarm execution has persisted status, validated outputs, final decisions, and validation metadata.
acceptanceCriteria: SQL migrations create agent_runs, agent_outputs, and bid_decisions with UUID primary keys and created_at timestamps.
agent_runs track status pending/running/succeeded/failed/needs_human_review, target tender/company, run configuration JSONB, error details, started_at, and completed_at.
agent_outputs store one immutable row per agent and round with output_type, validated JSONB payload, model metadata, timing/cost estimate fields, and validation errors.
bid_decisions link to the source agent_run and store the final validated decision JSON, verdict, confidence, and cited evidence IDs.
pytest and ruff check pass for any migration validation helpers or schema contract tests.
userStories.notes: Added agent audit migration for agent_runs, immutable agent_outputs keyed by role/round/output type, and bid_decisions with evidence_ids. Complete.

userStories.title: Add chunk evidence schema
userStories.description: As a developer, I need chunk and evidence tables so that tender text and company facts can become excerpt-level evidence with precise provenance.
acceptanceCriteria: SQL migrations create document_chunks with document_id, page_start, page_end, chunk_index, text, metadata JSONB, and nullable vector-ready embedding support.
SQL migrations create evidence_items with stable evidence_key, source_type, excerpt, normalized_meaning, category, confidence, and source metadata JSONB.
evidence_items source_type is limited to tender_document and company_profile for v1.
tender_document evidence requires document_id, chunk_id, page reference, excerpt, and source label metadata.
company_profile evidence requires company_id, field_path, excerpt, and source label metadata.
userStories.notes: Added document_chunks and evidence_items migrations with nullable vector embeddings and tender/company provenance constraints. Complete.

userStories.title: Define graph state schema
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a typed LangGraph state schema so that Bidded has a clear runtime source of truth before node ownership and routing are implemented.
acceptanceCriteria: A typed BidRunState defines run identity, company/tender/document IDs, run context, chunks, evidence board, scout output, motions, rebuttals, validation errors, retry counts, final decision, and status.
BidRunState distinguishes runtime control fields from persisted audit artifacts so implementation does not treat every field as mutable workflow memory.
BidRunState supports pending, running, succeeded, failed, and needs_human_review statuses consistently with agent_runs.
State serialization and deserialization tests cover an empty run, a run with evidence and motions, and a run with final decision state.
pytest and ruff check pass for graph state schema tests.
userStories.notes: Added Pydantic BidRunState with runtime/audit field groups and round-trip tests for empty, motion, and final decision states. Complete.

userStories.title: Enforce state ownership rules
userStories.description: As a developer, I need state ownership and mutation rules so that graph nodes cannot overwrite validated artifacts or each other's outputs.
acceptanceCriteria: Each graph node has documented read fields and owned write fields; nodes must not write outside their ownership.
Validated evidence board items, motions, rebuttals, validation errors, agent outputs, and final decisions are append-only artifacts unless a typed reducer explicitly merges parallel outputs.
Only runtime control fields such as status, current_step, retry_counts, last_error, and working retrieval results may be overwritten during a run.
Round 1 and Round 2 specialist outputs use reducers keyed by agent role so parallel writes cannot overwrite each other.
Tests prove owned write enforcement, append-only behavior, overwrite policy, and reducer behavior for parallel specialist outputs.
pytest and ruff check pass for state ownership tests.
userStories.notes: Added graph node contracts, append-only/write-once state updates, role-keyed reducers, and ownership tests. Complete.

userStories.title: Define agent tool policies
userStories.description: As a developer, I need explicit tool policies so that LLM agents produce validated artifacts while the orchestrator owns persistence and side effects.
acceptanceCriteria: Evidence Scout may call only bounded retrieval services scoped to the current agent_run, tender_id, and company_id, and may propose evidence candidates but does not directly mutate persisted evidence rows.
Specialist agents have read-only access to the shared evidence board and may write only their own motions, rebuttals, missing_info, and potential_evidence_gaps.
Judge may read validated evidence, motions, and rebuttals, and may write only the final decision artifact.
The orchestrator owns Supabase writes, status transitions, validation, and persistence of evidence_items, agent_outputs, and bid_decisions.
No LLM agent has arbitrary web search, filesystem access, code execution, direct database mutation, or permission to introduce new external sources during a run in v1.
userStories.notes: Added immutable agent tool policies for LLM denied tools, bounded retrieval, artifact access, and orchestrator side effects. Complete.

userStories.title: Define agent output schemas
userStories.description: As a developer, I need strict Pydantic output schemas so that motions, rebuttals, and Judge decisions are comparable, validated, and audit-friendly.
acceptanceCriteria: Schemas define agent roles, verdict values bid/no_bid/conditional_bid, confidence, evidence references, assumptions, missing_info, recommended_actions, and validation errors.
Round 1 motion schemas cover vote, top findings, role-specific risks or blockers, potential blockers, assumptions, missing_info, and recommended_actions.
Round 2 rebuttal schemas cover targeted disagreements, unsupported claims, blocker challenges, evidence references, and revised stance when applicable.

Judge decision schema covers verdict, confidence, vote_summary, disagreement summary, compliance_matrix, compliance_blockers, potential_blockers, risk_register, missing_info, recommended_actions, cited_memo, and evidence_ids.
pytest and ruff check pass for schema construction and serialization tests.
userStories.notes: Added strict Pydantic schemas for agent roles, votes, motions, rebuttals, Judge decisions, validation errors, and serialization tests. Complete.

userStories.title: Validate evidence claims
userStories.description: As a developer, I need evidence-claim validation so that material agent claims are grounded in evidence IDs rather than hidden assumptions.
acceptanceCriteria: Validation rejects material findings in motions, rebuttals, and Judge decisions when they omit evidence IDs.
Unsupported non-material points must be explicitly typed as assumptions, missing_info, or potential_evidence_gaps.
A claim may cite tender_document evidence, company_profile evidence, or both depending on whether it compares a requirement to company capability.
Validation errors are structured enough to support node retries and persisted agent_outputs validation_errors.
Tests cover valid cited claims, missing evidence IDs, assumptions, missing_info, and tender/company comparison claims.
userStories.notes: Added schema validation for resolved evidence IDs, typed evidence gaps, validation error codes, and tender/company comparison tests. Complete.

userStories.title: Seed demo company profile
userStories.description: As a bid manager, I need a seeded larger IT consultancy profile so that the swarm can compare tender requirements against realistic certifications, references, capacity, and economics.
acceptanceCriteria: A seed command or script creates exactly one demo company representing a larger IT consultancy rather than an SME.
Seed data includes structured certifications, public-sector references, case studies, CV summaries, revenue bands, delivery capacity, geographic availability, and simple cost/rate assumptions.
Company capabilities are persisted using core schema fields plus JSONB for fast-moving details.
Running the seed twice is idempotent and does not create duplicate demo companies.
pytest and ruff check pass for the seed logic.
userStories.notes: Added idempotent seed-demo-company CLI and deterministic larger consultancy payload with JSONB capability, reference, CV, and financial data. Complete.

userStories.title: Convert company facts to evidence
userStories.description: As a bid manager, I need seeded company facts converted to evidence items so that company capabilities are cited the same way as tender requirements.
acceptanceCriteria: A service converts seeded company certifications, references, case studies, CV summaries, revenue, capacity, geography, and economics into source_type=company_profile evidence_items.
Each company_profile evidence item includes company_id, field_path, source label, excerpt, normalized meaning, category, and confidence.
Conversion is idempotent and does not duplicate unchanged company evidence items.
Tests cover conversion for certification, reference, CV/capacity, revenue, and economics facts.
pytest and ruff check pass for company evidence conversion.
userStories.notes: Added deterministic company_profile evidence conversion and idempotent evidence_items upsert service for seeded company facts. Complete.

userStories.title: Register demo tender PDF
userStories.description: As an operator, I need to register a user-provided procurement PDF so that a real Swedish public procurement can be used as the hackathon tender fixture with English outputs.
acceptanceCriteria: A CLI command accepts a local text-PDF path, tender title, issuing authority, and optional procurement metadata.
CLI help or docs identify data/demo/incoming/Bilaga Skakrav.pdf as the preferred local gitignored demo input when present, without requiring it for automated tests.
The command uploads the PDF to the configured Supabase Storage bucket and records storage_path, checksum_sha256, content_type, and original filename in documents.
The command creates or updates a tender row linked to the uploaded procurement document and the demo company.
The command fails with a clear error when the file is missing, not a PDF, or Supabase credentials are absent.
pytest and ruff check pass for registration logic using a fixture or generated PDF and mocked Supabase Storage.
userStories.notes: Added register-demo-tender CLI plus injected Supabase Storage/tender/document registration tests and docs. Complete.

userStories.title: Create pending run context
userStories.description: As an operator, I need to create a pending agent run so that Lovable or the CLI can trigger the same Supabase-backed swarm workflow.
acceptanceCriteria: A CLI command or service creates an agent_runs row linked to the selected tender and demo company.
The run configuration records English input/output, Swedish public procurement context, active agent roles, and evidence-lock settings.
The command can create a pending run without executing any LLM or document processing step.
Creating a run validates that the tender, company, and procurement document exist.
pytest and ruff check pass for pending run creation tests.
userStories.notes: Added pending run service and CLI command with deterministic config and Supabase row validation. Complete.

userStories.title: Ingest PDF chunks
userStories.description: As a developer, I need text-PDF extraction and chunking so that tender documents become page-referenced chunks for retrieval and evidence extraction.
acceptanceCriteria: The document ingestion service extracts text from text-based PDF pages and explicitly treats OCR and DOCX as non-goals for this PRD.
Extracted text is split into deterministic chunks with document_id, page_start, page_end, chunk_index, text, and metadata.
documents.parse_status is updated to parsed when chunking succeeds and parser_failed with an error message when extraction fails.
Chunking preserves enough page reference metadata for later tender_document evidence_items to cite original PDF pages.
Tests cover successful text-PDF extraction, empty PDF text handling, and parser failure persistence.
pytest and ruff check pass for PDF ingestion logic.
userStories.notes: Added registered tender PDF ingestion with PyMuPDF extraction, deterministic page chunks, parser status persistence, and tests. Complete.

userStories.title: Add retrieval fallback
userStories.description: As a developer, I need retrieval that works without live embeddings so that the demo remains reliable while still being pgvector-ready.
acceptanceCriteria: Retrieval supports deterministic keyword or full-text scoring over document_chunks and returns top-K chunks with document/page metadata.
A mock embedding adapter produces deterministic vectors for tests without calling a hosted embedding provider.
Embedding configuration is optional; missing embedding settings never prevent keyword retrieval from working.
When embeddings are configured or mocked, retrieved chunk results include the retrieval method and score in metadata.
Tests prove retrieval returns relevant chunks through fallback search and through the mock embedding path.
pytest and ruff check pass for retrieval logic.
userStories.notes: Added document chunk retrieval with keyword fallback, optional mock embedding scoring, and top-K metadata tests. Complete.

userStories.title: Build tender evidence board
userStories.description: As a bid manager, I need validated tender evidence items so that specialist agents analyze cited procurement facts rather than free-form document summaries.
acceptanceCriteria: Evidence Scout or evidence extraction services propose tender evidence candidates from retrieved chunks.
The evidence validator accepts only tender_document evidence with document_id, chunk_id, page reference, excerpt, source label, category, and normalized meaning.
The orchestrator persists validated tender evidence_items; Evidence Scout does not directly mutate persisted evidence rows.
Every persisted tender evidence item receives a stable human-readable evidence key suitable for agent citations.
Tests cover candidate validation, duplicate prevention, stable evidence keys, and lookup by evidence key.
pytest and ruff check pass for tender evidence-board logic.
userStories.notes: Added tender candidate extraction, strict provenance validation, stable citation keys, idempotent upsert persistence, and evidence-key lookup. Complete.

userStories.title: Add graph routing shell
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a fixed LangGraph routing shell so that the bid/no-bid meeting simulation has explicit state transitions before real agent nodes are filled in.
acceptanceCriteria: Document registration, PDF ingestion/chunking, and evidence-board preparation are prerequisites before the LangGraph swarm starts; graph preflight verifies them but does not run ingestion.
The graph topology is fixed as preflight, Evidence Scout, parallel Round 1 specialists, Round 2 rebuttals, Judge, persist decision, and END.
Routing uses orchestrator-controlled conditional edges rather than LLM-selected handoffs.
Routing is documented as an explicit edge table covering preflight, Evidence Scout, Round 1 join, Round 2 join, Judge, persist decision, failed, needs_human_review, and END.
Conditional edges route invalid outputs to retry handling and missing inputs, unparsed documents, parser_failed documents, or empty evidence board to failed status.
Tests cover the mocked success path, invalid output path, missing/unparsed document path, empty evidence path, and final END path; pytest and ruff check pass.
userStories.notes: Added fixed LangGraph routing shell with preflight prerequisite checks, explicit edge table, mocked handlers, retry route, failed, needs_human_review, and END tests. Complete.

userStories.title: Implement Evidence Scout node
userStories.description: As a bid manager, I need an Evidence Scout node so that the swarm starts from cited procurement facts rather than unsupported opinions.
acceptanceCriteria: Evidence Scout retrieves relevant tender chunks and extracts the six-pack categories: deadlines, shall requirements, qualification criteria, evaluation criteria, contract risks, and required submission documents.
Evidence Scout writes only evidence-backed scout output and does not make bid/no-bid recommendations.
Scout output links every extracted fact to one or more evidence IDs from the validated evidence board.
The scout step persists its validated output as an agent_outputs row with role evidence_scout and round evidence.
Tests use mocked Claude output to prove unsupported extracted facts fail validation and do not persist as valid scout output.
pytest and ruff check pass for Evidence Scout logic.
userStories.notes: Implemented Evidence Scout schemas, six-pack retrieval request, evidence-board validation, and evidence_scout/evidence agent output append. Complete.

userStories.title: Implement specialist motions
userStories.description: As a bid manager, I need independent specialist motions so that Compliance, Win, Delivery/CFO, and Red Team reason from the same evidence without groupthink.
acceptanceCriteria: Compliance Officer, Win Strategist, Delivery/CFO Agent, and Red Team run from the same shared evidence board after Evidence Scout completes.
Round 1 specialists do not receive each other's motions or private context.
Each specialist motion includes verdict, confidence, evidence-backed findings, role-specific risks or blockers, assumptions, missing_info, and recommended_actions.
Compliance Officer is the only role that can mark formal compliance blockers as hard blocker candidates for automatic Judge gating.
Validated specialist motions persist as separate agent_outputs rows with round round_1_motion.
pytest and ruff check pass for mocked parallel motion orchestration.
userStories.notes: Implemented evidence-locked Round 1 specialist motion handling, validation, formal-blocker enforcement, and round_1_motion audit rows. Complete.

userStories.title: Implement focused rebuttals
userStories.description: As a bid manager, I need focused cross-critique so that major disagreements, weak evidence, and blocker disputes surface before the Judge decides.
acceptanceCriteria: Round 2 is the first point where specialists can read other agents' validated motions.
Each rebuttal reads the shared evidence board and all validated Round 1 motions.
Rebuttals target top disagreements, unsupported claims, blocker challenges, and material missing information rather than full pairwise reviews.
Red Team performs a second-round adversarial critique of the strongest bid arguments and any conditional-bid logic.
Validated rebuttals persist as separate agent_outputs rows with round round_2_rebuttal.
pytest and ruff check pass for mocked rebuttal orchestration.
userStories.notes: Implemented focused Round 2 rebuttal requests, validation, Red Team focus, and round_2_rebuttal audit rows. Complete.

userStories.title: Implement Judge decision
userStories.description: As a bid manager, I need a Judge node so that formal compliance gates, agent votes, disagreements, and evidence are converted into an auditable bid/no-bid/conditional decision.
acceptanceCriteria: Judge automatically gates to no_bid when validated formal compliance blockers are present.
When no hard compliance blocker exists, Judge may use evidence-backed discretion over specialist votes and must explain why it accepts or overrides the majority.

Judge output includes verdict, confidence, vote_summary, disagreement summary, compliance_matrix, compliance_blockers, potential_blockers, risk_register, missing_info, recommended_actions, cited_memo, and evidence_ids.

Judge uses conditional_bid when it can make a defensible bid recommendation with explicit next actions, and uses needs_human_review when critical missing or conflicting evidence prevents a defensible final verdict.
Final decisions persist to bid_decisions and link back to the source agent_run and relevant agent_outputs.
pytest and ruff check pass for mocked Judge scenarios including bid, no_bid, conditional_bid, and needs_human_review.
userStories.notes: Implemented Judge request validation, formal blocker no_bid gating, final_decision audit rows, and bid_decisions persistence payloads. Complete.

userStories.title: Add worker lifecycle CLI
userStories.description: As an operator, I need a local CLI worker so that Lovable or Supabase can create pending runs while the Python worker executes the long-running graph reliably.
acceptanceCriteria: A worker command can run a specified agent_run by ID or pick the oldest pending run for the demo company.
The worker transitions status pending to running to succeeded, failed, or needs_human_review with timestamps and error details.
The worker writes normalized outputs and metadata to agent_outputs and bid_decisions without storing raw full prompts as the default audit artifact.
The worker logs enough progress locally for demo operation while Supabase remains the source of truth for run state.
pytest and ruff check pass for lifecycle tests with Supabase and LLM calls mocked.

userStories.title: Add retry stop policy
userStories.description: As a developer, I need bounded retry and stop conditions so that the LangGraph worker cannot loop indefinitely or finish before the decision is valid.
acceptanceCriteria: Each LLM node has at most 2 retries after invalid JSON, schema validation failure, or evidence-reference validation failure; parallel Round 1 and Round 2 retries are scoped per agent role.
Judge runs only after all four required Round 1 motions and all four required Round 2 rebuttals are valid.
The graph reaches END only after a validated Judge decision is persisted to bid_decisions and agent_runs is marked succeeded.

The graph marks failed when required tender/company/document input is missing, the document is unparsed or parser_failed, the evidence board is empty, a required agent artifact remains invalid after retries, or persistence fails.
The graph may mark needs_human_review only when the run is technically valid but the persisted Judge artifact states that critical missing or conflicting evidence prevents a defensible final verdict.
Tests cover retry success, retry exhaustion, missing/unparsed document, empty evidence board, join conditions, needs_human_review, failed, and succeeded END paths; pytest and ruff check pass.

userStories.title: Test mocked end-to-end run
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a deterministic mocked end-to-end test so that the full evidence-locked swarm can be changed safely without live Claude or embedding calls.
acceptanceCriteria: An integration test seeds the demo company, registers a fixture tender document or fixture chunks, builds evidence, runs all swarm rounds, and persists a final bid_decision using mocked Claude outputs.
The mocked flow proves that unsupported material claims are rejected or moved to assumptions, missing_info, potential_evidence_gaps, or validation errors before final decision persistence.
The mocked flow proves that formal compliance blockers gate the final verdict while potential blockers from missing company evidence do not automatically gate.
The mocked flow proves agent_outputs contain Evidence Scout output, four Round 1 motions, four Round 2 rebuttals, and Judge output for a single agent_run.
The test suite does not require live Anthropic, live embeddings, OCR, DOCX, Supabase Auth, RLS, Next.js, React, or Lovable.
pytest and ruff check pass for the end-to-end suite.

userStories.title: Prepare Lovable handoff
userStories.description: As a founder, I need a Lovable-ready Supabase contract so that a thin demo UI can create runs and display evidence, motions, rebuttals, and decisions without owning agent logic.
acceptanceCriteria: A handoff document describes the Supabase tables, columns, statuses, and query patterns Lovable should use for a read-first demo UI.
The handoff explains that Lovable may create pending agent_runs and read results, but the Python CLI worker owns the long-running LangGraph/Claude execution.

The handoff includes recommended UI sections for run status, evidence board, Round 1 motions, Round 2 rebuttals, Judge decision, compliance matrix, risk register, missing information, and next actions.
A seed or fixture command can create at least one pending or completed demo run shape that Lovable can render without requiring live agent execution.
The handoff explicitly lists non-goals for this PRD: full Next.js/React UI, OCR, DOCX parsing, auth/RLS, real tender search, and live embedding-provider dependency.
pytest and ruff check pass after adding any handoff fixtures or helper scripts.

userStories.title: Add demo environment doctor
userStories.description: As an operator, I need a demo doctor command so that missing environment, Supabase, Storage, or Claude setup is detected before a live demo.
acceptanceCriteria: A CLI command checks required demo environment variables and reports missing values without printing secrets.
The command verifies Supabase database connectivity and confirms the expected demo tables are present.
The command verifies the configured Storage bucket is reachable with a mocked or live-safe read/write probe.
Anthropic connectivity is checked only when explicitly requested or when a key is present, and failures are reported as live-LLM unavailable rather than generic errors.
Tests mock external clients and cover success, missing env, missing table, missing bucket, and secret redaction; pytest and ruff check pass.

userStories.title: Seed replayable demo states
userStories.description: As an operator, I need replayable demo run fixtures so that the demo can show pending, successful, failed, and needs-human-review states without live agent execution.
acceptanceCriteria: A seed command creates deterministic demo company, tender, evidence, representative agent_runs, and agent_outputs or bid_decisions where applicable for pending, succeeded, failed, and needs_human_review states.
Seeded rows are scoped with explicit fixture metadata so the command never mutates real or manually created runs.
Running the seed twice is idempotent and does not duplicate fixture runs, evidence items, agent outputs, or decisions.
Seeded succeeded and needs_human_review runs contain valid evidence IDs and validated payload shapes compatible with existing Pydantic schemas.
Tests cover idempotence, fixture scoping, valid evidence references, and seeded run readability; pytest and ruff check pass.

userStories.title: Harden worker claiming
userStories.description: As an operator, I need safe worker claiming so that two local workers cannot execute the same pending agent run during a demo.
acceptanceCriteria: Worker startup claims a pending run atomically before execution and returns no run when another worker has already claimed it.
Terminal runs with status succeeded, failed, or needs_human_review cannot be claimed or executed again by the worker.
Claiming records started_at and worker metadata without changing validated agent outputs or bid decisions.
If SQL support is needed, a migration or helper function implements the atomic claim with deterministic contract tests.
Tests cover normal claim, double-claim, terminal-run rejection, and no-pending-run behavior; pytest and ruff check pass.

userStories.title: Add operator run controls
userStories.description: As an operator, I need local run status, retry, and stale-reset commands so that a failed or stuck demo can be recovered without editing Supabase by hand.
acceptanceCriteria: A run status command prints current status, timestamps, error details, output counts, decision presence, and last recorded step for a selected run.
A retry command creates a new pending run linked to the failed or needs_human_review source run instead of mutating immutable agent_outputs.
A reset-stale command marks old running runs as failed only when they exceed a configured age and records an explicit operator reason.
Commands refuse to mutate succeeded runs unless a documented force flag is provided, and force usage is recorded in metadata.
Tests cover status output, retry lineage, stale reset, terminal protections, and mocked persistence failures; pytest and ruff check pass.

userStories.title: Add live demo smoke command
userStories.description: As an operator, I need an opt-in smoke command so that the real Supabase and Storage demo path can be checked before presentation while normal tests stay deterministic.
acceptanceCriteria: A CLI command runs a bounded smoke flow covering demo seed, tender PDF registration, ingestion, evidence creation, pending run creation, worker execution, and decision readback.
The command accepts a local PDF path and falls back to generated or fixture data when the real gitignored demo PDF is absent.
The default smoke path uses mocked LLM output, while an explicit live-LLM flag enables real Claude calls for manual rehearsal.
Smoke output summarizes each step, terminal run status, decision verdict, evidence count, and any failure reason without printing secrets.
Automated tests mock Supabase, Storage, PDF processing, and Claude and cover PDF-present, PDF-absent, mocked-LLM, and live-LLM flag handling; pytest and ruff check pass.

userStories.title: Add demo step trace
userStories.description: As an operator, I need a compact per-run step trace so that demo failures can be understood without inspecting raw prompts or database internals.
acceptanceCriteria: The worker records a compact demo_trace in agent_runs metadata with step name, status, timestamps, duration, and short error code.
Trace entries never include raw prompts, secrets, full PDF text, or private free-form context.
Run status output can display the trace in a verbose mode with the latest failed or incomplete step highlighted.
Trace recording is append-only for completed steps and preserves existing run metadata.
Tests cover successful traces, failed traces, redaction, append-only behavior, and verbose status rendering; pytest and ruff check pass.

userStories.title: Export decision bundle
userStories.description: As an operator, I need a decision export so that the final recommendation, evidence, risks, and actions can be shown even if the UI is unavailable.
acceptanceCriteria: A CLI export command writes a selected run as Markdown and JSON using bid_decisions, agent_outputs, and evidence_items.
The export includes verdict, confidence, cited memo, evidence keys, compliance blockers, potential blockers, risk register, missing information, and recommended actions.
Exports fail with a clear message when the run has no persisted final decision.
Markdown output is readable without database access and JSON output preserves stable keys for downstream tooling.
Tests cover succeeded export, needs_human_review export, missing-decision failure, evidence citation rendering, and deterministic output ordering; pytest and ruff check pass.

userStories.title: Write demo runbook
userStories.description: As an operator, I need a demo runbook so that setup, smoke checks, recovery, and fallback replay can be followed under hackathon time pressure.
acceptanceCriteria: A runbook documents pre-demo setup, required environment variables, Supabase migration expectations, Storage bucket setup, and secret-handling rules.
The runbook documents the normal live demo path using doctor, smoke, worker, status, and export commands.
The runbook documents fallback paths for missing PDF, unavailable Claude, Supabase failure, stuck running runs, and UI unavailability.
The runbook explicitly states that normal tests remain mocked and that live smoke is manual and opt-in.
pytest and ruff check pass after adding the documentation.

userStories.title: Define golden demo cases
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a small golden case set so that Bidded's core bid/no-bid behaviors can be regression-tested without procurement domain guesswork.
acceptanceCriteria: Golden cases are represented as deterministic fixtures with tender evidence, company evidence, expected verdict, expected blockers, expected missing_info, and required evidence references.

The initial set covers obvious bid, hard compliance no_bid, conditional_bid with next actions, needs_human_review from conflicting evidence, missing company evidence, and unsupported agent claim rejection.
Each case explains why the expected outcome follows from the fixture evidence and Bidded decision rules rather than undocumented domain judgment.
Fixtures avoid private customer data and do not require live Claude, live Supabase, OCR, DOCX, or a real tender PDF.
Tests validate fixture schema, unique case IDs, expected verdict values, and presence of required evidence references; pytest and ruff check pass.

userStories.title: Add eval runner CLI
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an eval runner so that golden cases can be executed consistently after prompt, schema, retrieval, or orchestration changes.
acceptanceCriteria: A CLI command runs all golden cases or a selected case ID and returns a non-zero exit code when any required expectation fails.
The runner can execute with recorded or mocked agent outputs without calling live Claude or live Supabase.
The runner reports expected versus actual verdict, missing required blockers, unexpected hard blockers, validation errors, and evidence-reference failures.
Eval results are deterministic and can be written to JSON for later comparison.
Tests cover all-cases execution, selected-case execution, pass result, fail result, and JSON output; pytest and ruff check pass.

userStories.title: Score evidence coverage
userStories.description: As a developer, I need evidence coverage scoring so that golden evals can detect unsupported material claims and weak citation behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: An eval scorer computes citation coverage for material findings, blockers, Judge decisions, risk register entries, and recommended actions.
The scorer distinguishes tender_document evidence, company_profile evidence, and comparison claims that require both when both are available.
Unsupported material claims are counted separately from assumptions, missing_info, and potential_evidence_gaps.
Coverage output includes per-case score, unsupported claim count, missing citation details, and pass/fail threshold.
Tests cover full coverage, missing tender citation, missing company citation, unsupported claim, and allowed missing_info; pytest and ruff check pass.

userStories.title: Add verdict regression checks
userStories.description: As a developer, I need verdict regression checks so that core decision outcomes do not silently drift after implementation or prompt changes.
acceptanceCriteria: Golden cases define allowed verdicts and any required hard blocker, missing_info, or recommended_action expectations.
The eval runner fails when a case returns a verdict outside its allowed set.
Hard compliance blocker cases require no_bid regardless of other specialist votes.
Missing company evidence cases must not fail solely because evidence is absent when the expected rule is missing_info or human review.
Tests cover stable verdict pass, verdict drift failure, compliance blocker override, and missing-evidence non-gating behavior; pytest and ruff check pass.

userStories.title: Track prompt schema versions
userStories.description: As a developer, I need prompt, schema, retrieval, and model version metadata so that changed eval outcomes can be explained after future iterations.
acceptanceCriteria: Agent outputs or run metadata record prompt_version, schema_version, retrieval_version, model_name, and eval_fixture_version when available.
Version metadata is populated for mocked eval runs and normal worker runs without requiring live Claude.
The eval runner includes version metadata in JSON results and summary output.
Missing version metadata is surfaced as a warning in eval output but does not block non-eval worker execution.
Tests cover metadata population, warning behavior, JSON serialization, and backwards-compatible loading of older runs without version fields; pytest and ruff check pass.

userStories.title: Diff normalized decisions
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a normalized decision diff so that baseline and candidate runs can be compared without noisy prose diffs.
acceptanceCriteria: A diff command compares two eval results or persisted runs by verdict, confidence, blockers, risks, missing_info, recommended_actions, and cited evidence keys.
The diff ignores non-material prose ordering where normalized structured fields are unchanged.
The command emits human-readable text and JSON output with added, removed, and changed decision elements.
A non-zero exit code is returned when material decision fields differ and a strict flag is used.
Tests cover identical decisions, changed verdict, changed blockers, changed evidence keys, prose-only differences, and strict-mode exit codes; pytest and ruff check pass.

userStories.title: Add adversarial fixtures
userStories.description: As a developer, I need adversarial procurement fixtures so that evals exercise subtle evidence, compliance, and missing-information failures before real demos.
acceptanceCriteria: Adversarial fixtures cover near-miss certifications, hidden shall requirements, stale company evidence, conflicting deadlines, weak margin, and Red Team blocker challenges.
Each fixture has a documented expected behavior using Bidded rules and avoids requiring external legal or procurement expertise to interpret.
The eval runner can include or exclude adversarial fixtures through a CLI flag or fixture group selector.
Adversarial fixtures use only synthetic or repo-safe data and do not require private tenders or customer information.
Tests validate fixture grouping, expected outcome metadata, and at least one failing-output example for each adversarial category; pytest and ruff check pass.

userStories.title: Export eval report
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an eval report export so that golden case quality can be reviewed after local runs or before demos.
acceptanceCriteria: The eval runner can export Markdown and JSON reports with case status, expected versus actual verdict, evidence coverage score, unsupported claim count, and validation errors.
Reports include aggregate pass rate, failed case list, version metadata, and runtime summary.
Markdown reports are readable in a terminal or repository viewer, while JSON reports preserve stable keys for automation.
Report export is deterministic for the same eval input and output directory.
Tests cover all-pass report, mixed-pass report, JSON schema shape, Markdown content, and deterministic ordering; pytest and ruff check pass.

userStories.title: Compare live and mock evals
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an opt-in live-versus-mock eval comparison so that live Claude behavior can be rehearsed without making it a normal test dependency.
acceptanceCriteria: An eval command can run selected golden cases with mocked outputs and, when explicitly requested, live Claude outputs.
Live eval mode requires explicit confirmation or flag and never runs during normal pytest or default eval commands.
The comparison report highlights verdict differences, validation failures, evidence coverage changes, latency, and token or cost metadata when available.
If Anthropic credentials are absent, live comparison exits with a clear unavailable status while mock evals still run.
Tests mock the live client and cover disabled-by-default behavior, missing credentials, successful comparison, validation failure comparison, and report output; pytest and ruff check pass.
